---
title: Browser support
---

<!-- This file is generated by `packages/svelte/scripts/generate-browser-support.js`.
     Do not edit it by hand — your changes will be overwritten on the next release. -->

The table below shows the minimum browser versions in which Svelte's runtime, and the JavaScript the compiler emits from your `.svelte` files, are expected to work. It is regenerated on every release by scanning the actual code Svelte ships against the [`web-features`](https://github.com/web-platform-dx/web-features) dataset.

<!-- generated-table:start -->

_Resolved Baseline target: **Baseline 2020**._

| Browser           | Minimum version |
| ----------------- | --------------- |
| Chrome / Edge     | 87              |
| Chrome (Android)  | 87              |
| Firefox           | 83              |
| Firefox (Android) | 83              |
| Safari            | 14              |
| Safari (iOS)      | 14              |
| Opera             | 73              |
| Opera (Android)   | 62              |
| Samsung Internet  | 14.0            |
| Android WebView   | 87              |

<!-- generated-table:end -->

These numbers describe what Svelte's output _requires_ in order to run — they are derived from the APIs the code uses, not from a list of browsers the team commits to testing.

## What is covered

- **Svelte's runtime.** The browser builds of `svelte`, `svelte/animate`, `svelte/attachments`, `svelte/easing`, `svelte/events`, `svelte/internal/client`, `svelte/legacy`, `svelte/motion`, `svelte/reactivity`, `svelte/reactivity/window`, `svelte/store` and `svelte/transition` are scanned in the form they reach end users.
- **Compiler output.** The JavaScript the Svelte compiler emits from your `.svelte` files is scanned against a fixture set drawn from the compiler's own snapshot tests, so the same DOM operations, bindings and transitions you use in your components are covered.

## What is not covered

- **Your own code** inside `<script>` blocks or `.svelte.js` files. If you use newer browser APIs the table will not reflect them — configure your own [browserslist](https://github.com/browserslist/browserslist) and polyfills accordingly.
- **SvelteKit**, adapters and build tooling. See the [SvelteKit docs](https://svelte.dev/docs/kit) for the browser support story there.
- **Internet Explorer 11.** Svelte's runtime relies on `Proxy`, which cannot be polyfilled. IE11 is not supported and there is no path to making it work.

## How this page stays accurate

The minimum versions can only move forward in a minor or major release, and any change is recorded in the [changelog](https://github.com/sveltejs/svelte/blob/main/packages/svelte/CHANGELOG.md). CI runs the same generator on every pull request — if a change introduces a newer browser API, the build fails until this page is regenerated in the same pull request.
